item.title: European Youth Foundation Living and Building Democracy Grants 2026: Funding for Youth Initiatives Promoting Democracy and Human Rights
item.dc:creator: Yvonne Appeynarh
item.description:
The European Youth Foundation Living and Building Democracy Grants 2026 provide funding for youth initiatives promoting democracy and human rights across Europe. Launched by the European Youth Foundation (EYF) of the Council of Europe, this call for proposals supports ad hoc youth initiatives that empower young people to strengthen democratic culture, promote human rights, develop […]

The post European Youth Foundation Living and Building Democracy Grants 2026: Funding for Youth Initiatives Promoting Democracy and Human Rights appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Youth Foundation Living and Building Democracy Grants 2026 provide funding for youth initiatives promoting democracy and human rights across Europe. Launched by the European Youth Foundation (EYF) of the Council of Europe, this call for proposals supports ad hoc youth initiatives that empower young people to strengthen democratic culture, promote human rights, develop critical thinking, and actively participate in democratic processes. Through this grant programme, eligible youth organisations can implement innovative projects that address contemporary democratic challenges such as misinformation, disinformation, fake news, barriers to voting, and limited participation in governance, while equipping young people with the knowledge, skills, and attitudes needed to become active democratic citizens.

To strengthen youth participation and democratic engagement across Europe, the European Youth Foundation (EYF) of the Council of Europe has launched the Call for Youth Initiatives “Living and Building Democracy” 2026. This funding opportunity is designed to support innovative ad hoc youth initiatives that empower young people to better understand democracy, develop critical thinking skills, promote human rights, and actively participate in democratic life.

The grant programme encourages youth organisations to design impactful projects that respond to real democratic challenges facing young people while promoting democratic values, civic participation, and human rights education.

Applications are now open for eligible youth organisations registered with the European Youth Foundation.

About the European Youth Foundation Living and Building Democracy Call

The “Living and Building Democracy” call is a special funding initiative established by the European Youth Foundation to encourage youth organisations to create practical projects that strengthen democratic culture among young people.

Rather than supporting general youth activities, this call specifically focuses on initiatives that respond to contemporary democratic challenges and equip young people with the knowledge, attitudes, and skills needed to actively participate in democratic societies.

The initiative recognizes that democracy is strengthened when young people understand their rights, think critically, participate responsibly, and contribute to decision-making processes within their communities.

By supporting youth-led initiatives, the European Youth Foundation seeks to promote active citizenship while reinforcing the values of democracy, human rights, inclusion, and participation across Europe.

Objectives of the Grant Programme

The Living and Building Democracy grant aims to support initiatives that contribute to several important objectives.

1. Increase Young People’s Understanding of Democracy

Projects should create opportunities for young people to:

  • Learn about democratic values.
  • Understand democratic institutions.
  • Explore how democracy functions in modern society.
  • Understand the importance of protecting democratic systems.

2. Promote Human Rights Education

Applicants are encouraged to design activities that help participants understand:

  • Fundamental human rights.
  • The relationship between democracy and human rights.
  • Why human rights are essential to democratic societies.
  • How human rights can be protected and promoted.

3. Develop Critical Thinking

The European Youth Foundation seeks initiatives that help young people:

  • Analyze information critically.
  • Question misinformation.
  • Recognize manipulation.
  • Make informed civic decisions.
  • Build democratic attitudes and values.

This objective is especially important within today’s digital environment where false information spreads rapidly.

4. Strengthen Democratic Culture

Successful projects should encourage young people to:

  • Respect democratic values.
  • Participate in civic life.
  • Engage in dialogue.
  • Promote inclusion.
  • Build trust within communities.
  • Encourage democratic participation both online and offline.

5. Equip Young People with Citizenship Skills

The programme also aims to provide participants with practical citizenship skills that enable them to:

  • Participate in democratic decision-making.
  • Promote human rights.
  • Engage in community initiatives.
  • Contribute to governance processes.
  • Become active and responsible citizens.

Democratic Challenges the Grant Seeks to Address

The European Youth Foundation particularly welcomes projects that respond to specific barriers preventing young people from participating fully in democratic life.

Examples include:

  • Misinformation
  • Disinformation
  • Fake news
  • Information manipulation
  • Political disengagement
  • Low voter participation
  • Barriers to voting
  • Limited youth participation in governance
  • Weak civic engagement
  • Challenges affecting democratic participation in digital spaces

Applicants are expected to clearly explain:

  • Which democratic challenge their project addresses.
  • Why the challenge is important.
  • How young people are affected.
  • How the proposed activities will overcome or reduce that challenge.
  • The expected impact of the initiative.

What Kind of Initiatives Can Be Supported?

The programme supports ad hoc youth initiatives that contribute directly to strengthening democracy and promoting human rights.

Eligible activities may include:

  • Youth workshops
  • Democracy education programmes
  • Civic engagement projects
  • Human rights campaigns
  • Public awareness activities
  • Community dialogue sessions
  • Critical thinking workshops
  • Digital citizenship initiatives
  • Youth participation events
  • Democratic leadership activities
  • Advocacy and awareness campaigns

Projects should demonstrate innovation, relevance, and measurable impact on young people’s democratic participation.

Benefits of the Grant

Successful applicants will benefit from:

  • Financial support for implementing youth initiatives.
  • Opportunity to strengthen democratic participation among young people.
  • Support for innovative civic engagement projects.
  • Promotion of human rights education.
  • Increased youth involvement in democratic processes.
  • Contribution to democratic resilience across Europe.
  • Recognition through support from the European Youth Foundation.

Who Should Apply?

This funding opportunity is intended for:

  • Youth organisations registered with the European Youth Foundation.
  • Youth organisations committed to democracy and human rights.
  • Civil society organisations working with young people.
  • Organisations promoting active citizenship.
  • Youth-led organisations implementing democratic participation projects.

Applicants must ensure that their proposed projects align with the objectives and priorities outlined in the call for proposals.

Application Requirements

Applicants should carefully review the official call documents before submitting their applications.

Applications will be assessed based on:

  • Eligibility criteria.
  • Exclusion criteria.
  • Award criteria.
  • Relevance of the proposed initiative.
  • Quality of the project design.
  • Expected impact.
  • Demonstrated response to identified democratic challenges.

The initiative should clearly explain how it intends to address a specific democratic issue affecting young people and provide practical solutions through its planned activities.

Visit Here for more details.

Application Timeline

Applications opened for submission from the end of July 2026.

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications is:

1 September 2026 at 23:59 CEST

Late applications will not be considered.

How to Apply

Interested youth organisations should review the official call for proposals, confirm their eligibility with the European Youth Foundation, and complete the required application forms before the application deadline.

Applicants are encouraged to carefully study all eligibility conditions, funding requirements, and evaluation criteria before preparing their proposals.

For more opportunities like this visit here.

Conclusion

The European Youth Foundation Living and Building Democracy Grants 2026 provide an important opportunity for youth organisations to design meaningful initiatives that strengthen democracy, promote human rights, and empower young people to become active citizens. By supporting projects that tackle misinformation, encourage civic participation, and build democratic culture, the programme contributes to creating more inclusive, resilient, and participatory societies across Europe.

Eligible youth organisations registered with the European Youth Foundation are encouraged to take advantage of this funding opportunity and submit high-quality proposals before the 1 September 2026 (23:59 CEST) deadline. Through innovative youth-led initiatives, organisations can make a lasting contribution to democracy, civic engagement, and human rights education while helping young people develop the skills needed to shape Europe’s democratic future.

Click HERE to apply

The post European Youth Foundation Living and Building Democracy Grants 2026: Funding for Youth Initiatives Promoting Democracy and Human Rights appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNDP Responsible AI Bootcamp 2026: Fully Funded Online Training on Human Rights Impact Assessment for AI Developers, Start-ups and Public Institutions
item.dc:creator: Yvonne Appeynarh
item.description:
The UNDP Responsible AI Bootcamp 2026 is a fully funded online training programme designed to help AI developers, start-ups, and public institutions strengthen their understanding and application of responsible artificial intelligence through the Human Rights Impact Assessment (HRIA) Toolkit. Organized by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), the bootcamp provides participants with practical experience in […]

The post UNDP Responsible AI Bootcamp 2026: Fully Funded Online Training on Human Rights Impact Assessment for AI Developers, Start-ups and Public Institutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNDP Responsible AI Bootcamp 2026 is a fully funded online training programme designed to help AI developers, start-ups, and public institutions strengthen their understanding and application of responsible artificial intelligence through the Human Rights Impact Assessment (HRIA) Toolkit. Organized by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), the bootcamp provides participants with practical experience in assessing AI systems against responsible AI standards, while receiving guidance from international experts. Participants will explore AI governance, human rights considerations, and real-world AI implementation through interactive sessions, confidential breakout workshops, and hands-on use of the HRIA Toolkit. Successful participants will also receive a Certificate of Participation upon completing the programme.

As organizations increasingly integrate AI into decision-making and service delivery, ensuring that these technologies are developed and deployed responsibly has become more important than ever. Recognizing this need, the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) is inviting applications for the Responsible AI Bootcamp 2026, an intensive online training designed to help organizations assess and improve the human rights impacts of their AI systems.

This unique learning opportunity moves beyond theoretical discussions about responsible AI and provides participants with practical experience using the Human Rights Impact Assessment (HRIA) Toolkit. Through expert-led sessions, confidential workshops, and personalized guidance, participants will evaluate their own AI systems against internationally recognized responsible AI principles.

Participation in the bootcamp is completely free, and successful participants will receive a Certificate of Participation upon completion.

About the UNDP Responsible AI Bootcamp

The UNDP Responsible AI Bootcamp is a specialized online training program created to help organizations transition from understanding responsible AI concepts to implementing them in practice.

Rather than focusing solely on AI ethics theory, the bootcamp allows participants to test and assess their own AI projects using the Human Rights Impact Assessment (HRIA) Toolkit.

Throughout the program, participants will work directly with international experts while exploring practical methods for identifying and mitigating potential risks associated with AI systems.

The bootcamp combines interactive learning, expert mentorship, and confidential group sessions where participants can openly discuss their AI projects in a secure environment.

What is the Human Rights Impact Assessment (HRIA) Toolkit?

One of the central components of the bootcamp is the Human Rights Impact Assessment (HRIA) Toolkit.

The HRIA Toolkit is designed to help organizations systematically evaluate whether their AI systems align with responsible AI standards while protecting human rights throughout the AI lifecycle.

Using the toolkit, participants will learn how to:

  • Identify potential human rights risks associated with AI systems.
  • Evaluate fairness and non-discrimination within AI models.
  • Assess transparency and accountability mechanisms.
  • Understand privacy and data protection considerations.
  • Improve governance practices for AI deployment.
  • Develop practical strategies for mitigating identified risks.
  • Strengthen compliance with international responsible AI standards.

Participants will also gain access to the HRIA online platform, which supports the assessment process throughout the bootcamp.

Further explanations would be found here

What Participants Will Learn

During the bootcamp, participants will gain practical knowledge and experience in responsible AI implementation.

Key learning areas include:

  • Responsible AI principles and governance
  • Human Rights Impact Assessment methodology
  • AI risk identification and mitigation
  • Responsible deployment of AI systems
  • Ethical AI decision-making
  • Human rights considerations in AI design
  • AI governance frameworks
  • Responsible innovation strategies
  • Practical application of the HRIA Toolkit
  • Expert feedback on participants’ own AI use cases

Bootcamp Structure

The program combines theoretical instruction with hands-on practical exercises.

Participants will take part in:

1. Foundational Responsible AI Session

The bootcamp begins with a comprehensive introduction to responsible AI principles, governance frameworks, and human rights considerations.

This session establishes the foundation necessary to conduct meaningful AI assessments.

2. Confidential Breakout Sessions

Participants will join confidential breakout rooms where they can directly apply the HRIA Toolkit to their own AI systems or projects.

These sessions provide:

  • Personalized guidance
  • Practical demonstrations
  • Individual feedback
  • Expert mentoring
  • Peer learning opportunities

Because discussions are confidential, participants can openly discuss their AI projects without concern about public disclosure.

3. Expert Support

International experts specializing in responsible AI, AI governance, and human rights will provide ongoing guidance throughout the program.

Participants will receive support in understanding both technical and policy-related aspects of responsible AI.

Sectors Covered

The HRIA Toolkit focuses on AI applications across several high-impact sectors, including:

  • Education
  • Employment
  • Healthcare
  • Public services

Participants working within these sectors will have the opportunity to evaluate how their AI systems affect individuals, institutions, and society.

Benefits of Participating

Selected participants will enjoy numerous professional and practical benefits, including:

  • Free participation in the entire bootcamp.
  • Practical training in responsible AI implementation.
  • Hands-on experience using the Human Rights Impact Assessment Toolkit.
  • Direct mentorship from international experts.
  • Access to the HRIA online platform.
  • Practical evaluation of participants’ own AI systems.
  • Improved understanding of AI governance and human rights.
  • Networking opportunities with professionals working in AI and public policy.
  • Enhanced organizational capacity for responsible AI deployment.
  • Certificate of Participation upon successful completion.

 

Who Should Apply?

The UNDP Responsible AI Bootcamp is intended for organizations and professionals actively working with AI technologies.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Technology companies
  • AI-focused start-ups
  • Public institutions
  • Individual professionals working with AI
  • Teams developing or deploying AI systems

Applicants should possess basic knowledge of:

  • Artificial Intelligence
  • AI governance
  • Legal and regulatory considerations relating to AI

Applications from both individuals and teams are welcome.

UNDP also strongly encourages female candidates to submit applications.

Want similar opportunities like this? Visit here

Languages

The bootcamp will be conducted in:

  • English

Additionally, the Human Rights Impact Assessment platform is currently available in:

  • English
  • French
  • Spanish
  • Russian
  • BCMS (Bosnian, Croatian, Montenegrin and Serbian)

Bootcamp Details

Programme Dates

  • 21–22 September 2026

Delivery Mode

  • Online via Zoom

Participation Fee

  • Free of charge

Cohort Size

  • Approximately 25–30 participants

Certificate

Participants who successfully complete the program will receive a Certificate of Participation.

Application Deadline

The deadline to submit applications is:

15 August 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early, as only a limited number of participants will be selected.

For more details click HERE

How to Apply

Applicants should complete the official UNDP Responsible AI Bootcamp application form before the application deadline.

They may also explore additional resources to better understand the Human Rights Impact Assessment Toolkit and online platform before participating.

Conclusion

As governments, businesses, and institutions continue to integrate artificial intelligence into everyday operations, responsible AI governance is becoming a global priority. The UNDP Responsible AI Bootcamp 2026 offers a valuable opportunity for organizations and professionals to move beyond theoretical discussions and gain practical experience in assessing AI systems through a human rights lens.

With expert guidance, access to internationally recognized assessment tools, hands-on evaluation of real AI projects, and a free Certificate of Participation, this bootcamp provides participants with practical skills that can strengthen responsible AI development and deployment within their organizations. Eligible applicants from the participating countries are encouraged to submit their applications before the 15 August 2026 deadline and take advantage of this unique professional development opportunity.

Ready to apply visit HERE

The post UNDP Responsible AI Bootcamp 2026: Fully Funded Online Training on Human Rights Impact Assessment for AI Developers, Start-ups and Public Institutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Literary Arts Fund 2026 Innovation Project Grants: Up to $100,000 Available for U.S. Nonprofit Literary Arts Organizations
item.dc:creator: Nana Safo
item.description:
The Literary Arts Fund has officially opened applications for its 2026 Innovation Project Grants, a major funding opportunity designed to strengthen the nonprofit literary arts sector across the United States. As part of its long-term commitment to investing in literary organizations, the fund has pledged to provide at least $50 million in support to the […]

The post Literary Arts Fund 2026 Innovation Project Grants: Up to $100,000 Available for U.S. Nonprofit Literary Arts Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Literary Arts Fund has officially opened applications for its 2026 Innovation Project Grants, a major funding opportunity designed to strengthen the nonprofit literary arts sector across the United States. As part of its long-term commitment to investing in literary organizations, the fund has pledged to provide at least $50 million in support to the nonprofit literary arts ecosystem over the next five years, with funding continuing through 2031.

The 2026 Innovation Project Grants are specifically designed to support bold, forward-thinking projects that address structural and infrastructure challenges affecting nonprofit literary arts organizations. Successful applicants can receive grants ranging from $25,000 to $100,000, with no matching funds required.

Organizations that work directly with adult creative writers now have the opportunity to secure funding that will help strengthen their operations, improve services, and create long-term impact across the literary arts community.

About the Literary Arts Fund

The Literary Arts Fund was established to strengthen and sustain the nonprofit literary arts sector across the United States.

Rather than distributing funding across every area of the arts, the Fund focuses specifically on organizations that help adult creative writers produce and share literary works. Its strategy prioritizes investments where they can create the greatest long-term impact.

Over the next five years, the organization intends to distribute at least $50 million through various grant opportunities before concluding its funding cycle in 2031.

Each year, the Fund offers two major grant opportunities:

  • Innovation Project Grants
  • General Operating Grants

While the General Operating Grant application for 2026 has already closed, applications remain open for the Innovation Project Grants.

What Are the 2026 Innovation Project Grants?

The Innovation Project Grants provide funding for new, one-time projects that address major operational, structural, or infrastructure challenges faced by nonprofit literary arts organizations.

Rather than funding routine activities, these grants are intended to support innovative solutions that produce lasting improvements for organizations and the broader literary arts ecosystem.

Projects should generate benefits that continue well beyond the grant period and ultimately strengthen organizations’ ability to serve creative writers.

The Fund expects the competition to be highly competitive due to the limited funding available.

Grant Value

Successful applicants may request funding between:

  • Minimum grant: $25,000
  • Maximum grant: $100,000

In total, the Literary Arts Fund will distribute $1 million through the 2026 Innovation Project Grant program.

One important advantage is that applicants are not required to provide matching funds, nor must they have additional project funding already secured.

Purpose of the Funding

Innovation Project Grants are intended to help nonprofit literary organizations solve important challenges that affect their long-term sustainability.

Projects may focus on:

  • Organizational infrastructure improvements
  • New systems and processes
  • Innovative collaborative initiatives
  • Structural improvements
  • Capacity-building efforts
  • Long-term solutions benefiting literary arts organizations

Projects may also involve collaboration between multiple eligible nonprofit organizations.

Who Is Eligible?

To qualify, applicants must be independent nonprofit literary arts organizations, publishers, or publications located in the United States.

Their primary mission must directly support adult creative writers by helping them create and share literary works.

Eligible organizations typically support writers through activities such as:

  • Public literary events
    • Festivals
    • Readings
    • Open mic events
    • Spoken word performances
    • Literary conversations
    • Lectures
  • Publishing opportunities
    • Books
    • Chapbooks
    • Literary journals
    • Magazines
    • Digital publications
    • Literary websites
    • Zines
  • Professional development
    • Writing residencies
    • Retreats
    • Workshops
    • Mentorship programs
  • Audience development
    • Book reviews
    • Interviews
    • Podcasts
    • Online archives
    • Feature articles
  • Recognition of literary achievement
    • Awards
    • Fellowships
    • Literary prizes

Additional Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must also satisfy all of the following requirements:

1. Organizational History

Organizations must have existed for at least three years before applying.

For fiscally sponsored organizations, eligibility is determined using the date of the fiscal sponsorship agreement.

2. Leadership Stability

Applicants must have an Executive Director who has served continuously for at least one full year.

3. Financial Stability

Applicants must have:

  • Annual operating budget of at least $50,000
  • Budget excludes in-kind or non-cash contributions

For fiscally sponsored organizations, the applicant organization—not the fiscal sponsor—must meet this requirement.

Organizations That Are Not Eligible

The Literary Arts Fund does not currently support:

  • Screenwriting organizations
  • Playwriting organizations
  • Oral storytelling nonprofits
  • Writing therapy organizations
  • University or college departments
  • University-sponsored projects
  • Organizations primarily supporting young writers
  • Arts education organizations focused on K–12 students
  • Literacy organizations that teach reading or writing skills
  • Academic libraries
  • School libraries
  • Public libraries
  • Research libraries
  • Trade associations serving nonprofits

Collaborative Applications

Organizations may submit collaborative proposals.

Important rules include:

  • Only one organization may serve as the lead applicant.
  • The lead organization submits the application.
  • Collaborators may receive sub-awards if included in the approved project budget.
  • Every collaborating organization receiving funds must independently satisfy all eligibility requirements.
  • The lead applicant is responsible for verifying collaborator eligibility.

Restrictions for Previous Grant Recipients

Organizations that received a 2026 General Operating Grant cannot independently apply for an Innovation Project Grant during the same year.

However, they may:

  • Participate as collaborators
  • Receive project sub-awards
  • Join collaborative applications led by another eligible organization

Project Timeline

Approved projects must:

  • Begin after January 1, 2027
  • Finish on or before December 31, 2027

Grant funds must only be used during the approved project period.

Payment Structure

Successful applicants will receive their grant funding as a single payment.

For collaborative projects:

  • The lead applicant receives the full grant.
  • The lead applicant distributes any approved sub-awards to collaborating organizations.

Application Process

Applications are submitted online through Submittable.

Applicants should prepare all required materials before beginning the submission process.

Organizations wishing to revise an application after submission must:

  1. Withdraw the original application.
  2. Submit an entirely new application before the deadline.

For more information, visit Literary Arts Fund 2026 Innovation Project website HERE

Use of Artificial Intelligence

The Literary Arts Fund clearly states that applicants may not use generative AI to produce any portion of the application narrative.

Organizations should prepare all narrative responses independently.

Application Deadline

Applications for the 2026 Innovation Project Grants opened on June 8, 2026.

Application Deadline: August 17, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit well before the deadline to avoid technical issues or last-minute errors.

      APPLY HERE

Key Dates

  • By May 30, 2026 – General Operating Grant applicants notified
  • June 8, 2026 – Innovation Project Grant applications opened
  • August 17, 2026 – Innovation Project Grant application deadline
  • By December 31, 2026 – Successful applicants notified
  • January 2027 – Expected opening of the next General Operating Grant cycle

Available Applicant Resources

To help applicants prepare strong proposals, the Literary Arts Fund offers several free resources, including:

  • Innovation Project Grant information session recording
  • Application preparation guide
  • Budget best practices webinar
  • Budget templates and sample budgets
  • Field Scan research presentation
  • Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
  • Submittable technical support resources

These materials are intended to improve application quality and help organizations better understand the grant process.

For more grant opportunities visit OFY Website HERE

Why This Grant Matters

Many nonprofit literary organizations face operational and infrastructure challenges that limit their ability to support writers effectively.

The Innovation Project Grant program seeks to fund projects that create lasting improvements rather than temporary solutions.

By investing in stronger organizational systems, collaborative partnerships, and innovative approaches, the Literary Arts Fund hopes to strengthen the entire literary arts ecosystem while expanding opportunities for adult creative writers across the United States.

Conclusion

The Literary Arts Fund 2026 Innovation Project Grants present an outstanding funding opportunity for eligible U.S.-based nonprofit literary arts organizations seeking to implement transformative projects. With grants ranging from $25,000 to $100,000, no matching fund requirement, and support for collaborative initiatives, the program provides organizations with the resources needed to address significant structural challenges and build lasting impact. Interested organizations should carefully review all eligibility requirements, prepare their applications independently, and submit them before the August 17, 2026 deadline to maximize their chances of securing funding.

The post Literary Arts Fund 2026 Innovation Project Grants: Up to $100,000 Available for U.S. Nonprofit Literary Arts Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The Adidas Foundation Expression of Interest 2026: Up to €6 Million Available for Strategic Partners in the New Recovery Through Sport Program
item.dc:creator: Fridah Kamuri
item.description:
Organizations working at the intersection of humanitarian response, mental health and psychosocial support (MHPSS), child protection, and sport for development now have a unique opportunity to partner with the adidas Foundation through its newly launched Recovery Through Sport Program. Rather than seeking traditional project proposals, the Foundation is inviting experienced organizations to become long-term strategic […]

The post The Adidas Foundation Expression of Interest 2026: Up to €6 Million Available for Strategic Partners in the New Recovery Through Sport Program appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working at the intersection of humanitarian response, mental health and psychosocial support (MHPSS), child protection, and sport for development now have a unique opportunity to partner with the adidas Foundation through its newly launched Recovery Through Sport Program. Rather than seeking traditional project proposals, the Foundation is inviting experienced organizations to become long-term strategic partners in designing and implementing an innovative global initiative that supports children and young people recovering from crises and displacement.

With a total funding commitment of €6 million, the adidas Foundation plans to select one to three strategic partners that will collaboratively develop programming, learning frameworks, funding structures, and implementation approaches over an initial period of more than three years.

If your organization has demonstrated expertise in humanitarian programming, mental health and psychosocial support, safeguarding, and sport or physical activity, this could be one of the most significant partnership opportunities currently available in the global development and humanitarian sector.

About the adidas Foundation Recovery Through Sport Program

The Adidas Foundation is expanding its impact through the introduction of Recovery Through Sport, a new program area designed to help children and young people rebuild their well-being after experiencing crises, conflict, disasters, or forced displacement.

The Foundation recognizes that sport is far more than recreation. When implemented under the right conditions, sports and structured physical activity can help young people restore:

  • Emotional well-being
  • Self-confidence
  • Social connections
  • A sense of routine
  • Feelings of safety
  • Community belonging
  • Personal agency
  • Positive participation

However, the Foundation also emphasizes that sport is not intended to replace humanitarian assistance. Instead, it complements recovery efforts where appropriate safeguards, expertise, and community conditions exist.

Why the adidas Foundation Is Looking for Strategic Partners

Unlike conventional grant calls that require applicants to submit complete project proposals, this Expression of Interest focuses on identifying organizations with existing expertise and long-term capacity.

The adidas Foundation is intentionally starting with partners before defining projects because it believes sustainable solutions should be co-created with organizations that understand local realities.

Selected organizations will work closely with the Foundation to:

  • Design future programming
  • Develop funding models
  • Shape implementation strategies
  • Build evidence on effective approaches
  • Identify suitable implementation contexts
  • Test and improve interventions over time

This collaborative approach ensures that programs respond directly to real-world humanitarian needs rather than predetermined project concepts.

Background Research Behind the Program

Before launching Recovery Through Sport, the adidas Foundation undertook extensive groundwork.

Working alongside an independent consortium of experts in:

  • Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS)
  • Sport for Development
  • Physical Activity
  • Humanitarian Programming

The foundation reviewed existing research and consulted organizations working across humanitarian and development sectors.

The consultation revealed several important findings.

Sport Can Support Recovery

Research shows that carefully designed sport and physical activity programs can help children and young people recovering from crisis by strengthening:

  • Emotional resilience
  • Social relationships
  • Daily routines
  • Community participation
  • Psychological well-being

Significant Evidence Gaps Remain

Despite growing interest in sport-based recovery interventions, evidence remains limited, particularly in:

  • Low-resource humanitarian settings
  • Crisis-affected communities
  • Displacement contexts
  • Refugee environments

The adidas Foundation therefore wants its strategic partnerships to contribute not only to programming but also to expanding global evidence on what works.

What Makes This Opportunity Different?

This opportunity differs from traditional funding calls in several important ways.

1. It Is Not a Traditional Grant Competition

Applicants are not expected to submit detailed project proposals.

Instead, organizations are invited to demonstrate:

  • Existing expertise
  • Organizational capacity
  • Operational systems
  • Experience working in complex environments
  • Willingness to innovate

2. Long-Term Collaboration

Rather than funding isolated short-term projects, the Foundation seeks partnerships lasting at least three years, with potential for future expansion.

3. Co-Creation

Selected organizations will jointly develop:

  • Program design
  • Learning priorities
  • Funding arrangements
  • Evidence-building approaches
  • Operational strategies

4. Strong Focus on Learning

The Foundation wants every partnership to generate valuable evidence that improves humanitarian recovery through sport.

The Kind of Partners the adidas Foundation Is Looking For

The Foundation seeks organizations that already possess strong operational experience and are ready to strengthen and expand their work.

Ideal partners should demonstrate experience in areas such as:

  • Humanitarian response
  • Crisis recovery
  • Forced displacement
  • Child protection
  • Mental health and psychosocial support
  • Sport for development
  • Physical activity programming
  • Community-based interventions

Organizations should also have:

  • Robust safeguarding systems
  • Strong organizational governance
  • Experience working in complex operational environments
  • Capacity to deliver large-scale programming
  • A culture of learning and adaptation
  • Willingness to collaborate over the long term

Expression of Interest Focus Areas

The Foundation wants to understand what organizations already bring rather than asking them to create entirely new concepts.

Applicants should be prepared to demonstrate:

  • Existing recovery practice
  • Mental health and psychosocial support expertise
  • Safeguarding policies and systems
  • Operational reach
  • Sport or physical activity experience
  • Organizational learning culture
  • Readiness for long-term collaboration

Key Funding Information

The Recovery Through Sport initiative includes substantial financial support.

Total Funding

€6,000,000

This represents the total funding commitment across all selected strategic partners and future program activities.

Number of Strategic Partners

Between:

  • One
  • Two
  • Three

organizations will ultimately be selected.

Partnership Duration

The initial partnership will last more than three years with opportunities for continued collaboration beyond that period.

Benefits of Becoming an adidas Foundation Strategic Partner

Organizations selected through this Expression of Interest will benefit from:

  • Multi-year funding instead of short project cycles
  • Collaborative program design
  • Long-term strategic partnership
  • Joint development of implementation approaches
  • Access to evidence-informed frameworks
  • Opportunities to contribute to global learning on recovery through sport
  • Close collaboration with the adidas Foundation
  • Participation in shaping an emerging global program area
  • Strong emphasis on safeguarding and quality programming
  • Potential for long-term growth beyond the initial partnership period

Key Dates

Activity Date
Expression of Interest opens 15 July 2026
Submission deadline 30 August 2026 (11:59 PM CEST)

How to Apply

Interested organizations should carefully review the complete Expression of Interest Guidelines before preparing their submission.

The guidelines include:

  • Complete eligibility requirements
  • Partner profile
  • Submission questions
  • Selection criteria
  • Application process
  • Timeline
  • Additional process details

Applications must be submitted before the closing deadline.

APPLY HERE

What Applicants Should Know Before Applying

Applicants should note that:

  • This is a partner selection process.
  • It is not a request for project proposals.
  • Programming will be designed collaboratively.
  • Contexts for implementation will be jointly identified.
  • The Foundation prioritizes evidence, safeguarding, and quality implementation.
  • Long-term collaboration is central to this initiative.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Children and young people living through conflict, disasters, displacement, and humanitarian emergencies often experience profound disruptions to their emotional well-being, education, social relationships, and daily routines.

Recovery Through Sport seeks to explore how structured sport and physical activity can help restore normalcy while complementing broader humanitarian and psychosocial interventions.

By investing in experienced organizations and emphasizing collaborative learning, the adidas Foundation aims to strengthen global evidence on sport-based recovery interventions and develop sustainable approaches that can benefit vulnerable communities worldwide.

Visit HERE for more information about the Adidas Foundation Expression of Interest 2026

Conclusion

The adidas Foundation’s Recovery Through Sport Expression of Interest 2026 represents an important opportunity for organizations working in humanitarian response, child protection, mental health, psychosocial support, and sport for development. Rather than funding isolated projects, the Foundation is investing in long-term strategic partnerships built on trust, evidence, collaboration, and shared learning.

Organizations with proven operational experience, strong safeguarding systems, and a commitment to improving the lives of children and young people affected by crisis are encouraged to review the guidelines and submit their Expression of Interest before the deadline. Successful partners will play a central role in shaping an innovative global program that seeks to harness the power of sport to support recovery, resilience, and hope for young people facing some of the world’s most challenging circumstances.

Application Deadline: 30 August 2026 at 11:59 PM CEST

For more Grant opportunities, visit OFY HERE

The post The Adidas Foundation Expression of Interest 2026: Up to €6 Million Available for Strategic Partners in the New Recovery Through Sport Program appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026: Apply for the Virtual En-ROADS Climate Leadership Training
item.dc:creator: Ning Ma
item.description:
Applications are open for the Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026, a one-month virtual training programme designed to prepare professionals, educators, policymakers, business leaders and civil society practitioners to facilitate interactive, science-based climate events using the En-ROADS Climate Solutions Simulator. The live cohort will run from 4 August to 4 September 2026, combining the self-paced Mastering En-ROADS […]

The post Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026: Apply for the Virtual En-ROADS Climate Leadership Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are open for the Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026, a one-month virtual training programme designed to prepare professionals, educators, policymakers, business leaders and civil society practitioners to facilitate interactive, science-based climate events using the En-ROADS Climate Solutions Simulator.

The live cohort will run from 4 August to 4 September 2026, combining the self-paced Mastering En-ROADS online course with twice-weekly Zoom sessions led by members of the Climate Interactive team and other climate-engagement experts.

Participants will learn how to use En-ROADS to explain complex climate and energy systems, explore high-impact climate solutions and lead workshops, simulations and role-playing activities for decision-makers in government, business, finance, education and civil society.

Applications for the August 2026 cohort close on 2 August 2026.

The programme requires an estimated commitment of four to six hours per week. No previous experience with En-ROADS is required, and tuition is donation-based. Participants who cannot afford the suggested contribution may still access the training free of charge.

About the Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026

The Climate Ambassador Accelerator is an intensive virtual professional development programme focused on climate education, systems thinking and practical facilitation.

It combines two forms of learning:

  1. Self-paced study through the Mastering En-ROADS course
  2. Live online sessions featuring expert presentations, questions and answers, facilitation guidance and group discussions

The training is intended to help participants move beyond simply understanding climate policy. It prepares them to engage audiences in structured conversations about climate solutions, energy systems, land use, carbon removal, electrification, economic growth and other forces shaping the future of the climate.

Participants will learn how to facilitate events for audiences such as:

  • Government officials and policymakers
  • Corporate executives and business leaders
  • Investors and finance professionals
  • University students and academic institutions
  • Civil society organisations
  • Community leaders
  • Climate networks
  • Professional associations
  • Educational institutions

By the end of the programme, participants should be able to confidently organise and facilitate their own En-ROADS workshops, Climate Action Simulation games and educational activities.

What Is En-ROADS?

En-ROADS is an interactive climate policy simulation developed by Climate Interactive and the MIT Sloan School of Management.

The simulator allows users to explore how different policy choices may affect global temperature outcomes, energy systems, land use and emissions.

Participants can test measures related to areas such as:

  • Renewable energy
  • Coal, oil and natural gas
  • Energy efficiency
  • Electrification
  • Carbon pricing
  • Deforestation
  • Afforestation
  • Methane and other greenhouse gases
  • Population growth
  • Economic growth
  • Technological carbon removal
  • Industry
  • Waste
  • Agricultural emissions
  • Transport and buildings

Instead of presenting climate policy as a fixed list of recommendations, En-ROADS allows participants to experiment with different combinations of solutions and observe how those choices interact.

According to the programme information, En-ROADS has been used by more than 1.5 million people, including over 25,000 leaders from business, government and higher education programmes.

Purpose of the Climate Ambassador Accelerator

Climate change discussions often involve complicated scientific, political and economic questions. Even well-informed audiences may struggle to understand which solutions have the greatest potential impact and how different climate interventions influence one another.

The Climate Ambassador Accelerator is designed to help participants make these discussions more accessible, interactive and actionable.

The programme prepares facilitators to guide audiences through questions such as:

  • Which climate solutions have the greatest potential to reduce future warming?
  • How do energy supply and energy demand interact?
  • What role can electrification play in reducing emissions?
  • How significant are land-use changes and deforestation?
  • What are the limits and opportunities of carbon removal technologies?
  • How do economic growth and population affect emissions?
  • Why are some apparently promising climate solutions less effective than expected?
  • What combination of policies is required to achieve major temperature reductions?

Participants will learn not only how to operate the simulator, but also how to explain its results, manage group discussions and adapt events to different audiences.

Who Should Join the Climate Ambassador Accelerator?

The programme is especially suitable for people who work with decision-makers in:

  • Public policy
  • Government
  • Business
  • Finance
  • Higher education
  • Schools
  • Civil society
  • Sustainable development
  • Climate advocacy
  • Environmental education
  • Corporate sustainability
  • Community engagement

It may also be valuable for professionals who regularly organise workshops, training sessions, public consultations, strategic discussions or educational events.

The training may be a good fit for applicants who:

  • Want to make climate conversations practical and action-oriented
  • Work with audiences that influence policy, investment, education or organisational strategy
  • Want to lead interactive, science-based climate workshops
  • Are interested in climate systems and climate policy
  • Want to add climate facilitation to their professional skills
  • Can commit four to six hours per week for approximately one month
  • Have at least intermediate English proficiency
  • Are prepared to participate in a virtual international learning environment

No previous experience with En-ROADS is required.

Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026 Training Dates

The main programme will run from:

4 August to 4 September 2026

Course modules will become available on 3 August 2026, with access instructions sent to accepted participants by email.

The programme includes five weeks of scheduled learning activities, although the main live training period is described as a one-month programme.

Training Format

The Climate Ambassador Accelerator is delivered entirely online.

The format includes:

  • Short on-demand videos
  • Assigned readings
  • Ungraded quizzes
  • Self-paced online modules
  • Expert-led Zoom presentations
  • Questions and answers with the Climate Interactive team
  • Small-group or breakout discussions
  • Facilitation exercises
  • Recorded sessions for participants who cannot attend live

Participants can work through the online course material according to the weekly schedule.

Live sessions will be held on Tuesdays and Thursdays at 10:00 a.m. EDT, corresponding to GMT-4 during the programme period.

Each live session will last approximately 60 minutes and normally include:

  • Around 30 minutes of core expert content
  • Around 10 minutes of questions and answers
  • Around 20 minutes of group discussion

The live sessions are designed to complement the self-paced Mastering En-ROADS course rather than repeat it.

All sessions will be recorded and made available to participants.

Time Commitment

Participants should be prepared to dedicate approximately:

Four to six hours per week for one month

This includes:

  • Completing the weekly course modules
  • Watching training videos
  • Reviewing readings
  • Taking ungraded quizzes
  • Attending or watching the live sessions
  • Participating in discussions and facilitation exercises
  • Practising with the En-ROADS Simulator

Attending live is recommended, particularly when sessions include group discussions or breakout activities. However, participants who cannot attend every session can use the recordings.

Language Requirement

The training is delivered in English.

Intermediate English proficiency is recommended because participants will need to:

  • Follow expert presentations
  • Read course materials
  • Participate in discussions
  • Ask and answer questions
  • Practise facilitation
  • Communicate with participants from different countries
  • Lead future En-ROADS events

The programme information does not state that an English-language examination certificate is required.

Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026 Cost

The programme uses a donation-based tuition model.

Participants are strongly encouraged to contribute at least US$20, although larger donations are welcomed.

However, applicants who cannot afford the suggested donation may still access the training free of charge.

The En-ROADS Simulator and course materials are made available through support from Climate Interactive’s funders.

The programme should therefore not be described as a paid employment opportunity or a conventional fee-based professional course. It is a donation-supported training opportunity with free access available to those who need it.

Additional Regional Sessions

Participants in selected regions may receive access to additional sessions addressing local climate conditions and regional policy contexts.

The availability, timing and content of these sessions may vary by region.

These sessions may help participants connect global climate modelling with:

  • Regional energy systems
  • National climate commitments
  • Local development priorities
  • Climate-vulnerable communities
  • Regional policy debates
  • Local economic and social conditions

Skills Participants Will Develop

By the end of the programme, participants should be able to:

Explore Climate Solutions

Participants will understand how to use the En-ROADS Simulator to investigate a wide range of climate policies and technological solutions.

Navigate the En-ROADS Simulator

Participants will become familiar with:

  • Core controls
  • Policy sliders
  • Temperature outcomes
  • Energy graphs
  • Emissions data
  • Advanced charts and tools
  • Scenario comparison
  • Model assumptions

Explain System Dynamics

Participants will develop the ability to explain how energy, land use, emissions, population, economic growth and climate systems influence one another.

Facilitate Interactive Workshops

Participants will learn how to structure and lead En-ROADS Climate Workshops for decision-makers and professional audiences.

Lead Climate Action Simulation Games

The training will prepare participants to facilitate role-playing exercises in which audience members represent different sectors or stakeholder groups.

Adapt Events to Different Audiences

Participants will learn how to tailor climate events for:

  • Government leaders
  • Policymakers
  • Corporate boards
  • Business executives
  • Investors
  • Finance professionals
  • University students
  • Educators
  • Civil society organisations

Strengthen Climate Communication

Participants will practise translating complex climate science into understandable, relevant and actionable discussions.

Types of En-ROADS Events

There are three primary ways to use the En-ROADS Simulator with an audience.

1. En-ROADS Climate Workshop

A workshop allows participants to propose climate solutions and immediately observe how those choices affect the projected global temperature increase and other model outcomes.

The facilitator guides the discussion, tests proposals and helps the audience understand why some interventions have greater impact than others.

2. Climate Action Simulation Game

This is a role-playing activity in which participants represent different groups involved in climate decision-making.

Depending on the format, participants may represent governments, businesses, energy companies, civil society organisations, investors or other stakeholders.

The exercise helps participants understand negotiation, competing priorities and the need for coordinated climate action.

3. Student Assignment

Educators may use En-ROADS as part of a classroom assignment, course project or university curriculum.

Students can develop climate scenarios, test assumptions and analyse the interaction between different policies.

En-ROADS Climate Ambassador Certification

Completing the Climate Ambassador Accelerator does not automatically make a participant a certified En-ROADS Climate Ambassador.

To apply for certification, participants must:

  1. Complete the required training
  2. Facilitate at least two En-ROADS events
  3. Submit an application for Ambassador certification

Applications for certification are accepted on a rolling basis, and there is no stated certification deadline.

Participants will continue to have access to the training course after the live programme ends, allowing them to complete remaining materials and prepare their first events.

Benefits of Becoming a Certified En-ROADS Climate Ambassador

Certified Climate Ambassadors become part of a global community of climate facilitators.

Ambassadors may have opportunities to:

  • Participate in quarterly online gatherings
  • Attend special events
  • Collaborate with climate leaders from different regions
  • Receive support from the Climate Interactive team
  • Access selected professional opportunities
  • Expand their facilitation portfolio
  • Be listed in the Climate Ambassador directory
  • Make it easier for organisations to contact them
  • Use En-ROADS to inform policy and organisational strategy
  • Build professional credibility in climate education and engagement

Most Ambassadors are expected to identify and organise their own opportunities to facilitate events within their professional and community networks.

Climate Interactive may occasionally share facilitation opportunities, but certification does not guarantee employment, paid assignments or direct placement in events.

What Happens After the Live Training?

After the scheduled Zoom sessions conclude, participants will retain access to the online course.

There is no stated deadline for completing the remaining self-paced content.

Participants may use the post-training period to:

  • Review recorded sessions
  • Practise using the simulator
  • Complete unfinished modules
  • Design an En-ROADS workshop
  • Identify suitable audiences
  • Facilitate their first two events
  • Apply for Climate Ambassador certification

This flexible access is particularly useful for participants who cannot complete every activity during August.

Can Applicants With an Existing Mastering En-ROADS Account Apply?

Yes.

People who already have a Mastering En-ROADS account can apply to the Climate Ambassador Accelerator.

Accepted participants can sign in with their existing account after receiving programme instructions and register for the live sessions.

Having an existing account does not prevent participation.

What If a Participant Cannot Attend Every Live Session?

Live attendance is encouraged but not mandatory for every session.

All sessions will be recorded and made available to participants.

Participants should attend live where possible because group discussions and breakout activities may be difficult to reproduce through recordings. Nevertheless, the recorded format provides flexibility for applicants in different time zones or with professional commitments.

What If Someone Cannot Participate in August 2026?

Applicants who cannot join the August cohort may still take the self-paced Mastering En-ROADS course throughout the year.

The course is available year-round through Climate Interactive’s online learning platform.

However, participants taking only the self-paced course may not receive the same live expert sessions, cohort discussions and networking experience offered through the Climate Ambassador Accelerator.

Climate Ambassador Accelerator Information Session

An information session was scheduled for 7 July 2026 at 8:00 a.m. EDT.

The session was intended to help prospective applicants understand:

  • The training format
  • The time commitment
  • The En-ROADS Simulator
  • Certification requirements
  • Live-session expectations
  • Whether the programme matched their professional goals

Because the scheduled date has passed, prospective applicants should check whether a recording is available.

How to Apply for the Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026

Interested applicants must complete the official programme application to reserve a place in the August cohort.

Before applying, candidates should be prepared to provide information about:

  • Their professional background
  • Their involvement in climate, policy, business, finance, education or civil society
  • The audiences they work with
  • Their interest in En-ROADS
  • How they expect to use the training
  • Their ability to commit four to six hours per week
  • Their English-language ability
  • Their plans for facilitating future climate events

Applicants should submit the application before the deadline and monitor their email for acceptance instructions.

Programme modules will become available on 3 August 2026, making the period between the application deadline and the programme launch very short.

APPLY HERE

Application Deadline

Applications for the Climate Ambassador Accelerator close on:

2 August 2026

The training begins on 4 August 2026, while course modules and participant instructions are expected to become available on 3 August 2026.

Applicants should apply as early as possible rather than waiting until the final day.

Important Dates

  • Information session: 7 July 2026 at 8:00 a.m. EDT
  • Application deadline: 2 August 2026
  • Modules available: 3 August 2026
  • First live session: 4 August 2026
  • Main training period: 4 August–4 September 2026
  • Final scheduled week: 31 August–5 September 2026
  • Climate Ambassador certification: Rolling applications with no deadline

Important Points to Consider Before Applying

Prospective participants should understand that:

  • The programme is conducted in English.
  • Intermediate English proficiency is recommended.
  • The programme requires four to six hours per week.
  • Live sessions are scheduled according to Eastern Daylight Time.
  • The course is virtual.
  • Live attendance is recommended but recordings are available.
  • No prior En-ROADS experience is required.
  • Tuition is donation-based.
  • A contribution of at least US$20 is encouraged.
  • Participants who cannot afford the contribution may access the training free of charge.
  • Completing the course does not automatically result in Ambassador certification.
  • Certification requires facilitating at least two En-ROADS events.
  • Certification does not guarantee employment or paid facilitation opportunities.

Conclusion

The Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026 offers an accessible and practical route for professionals who want to lead stronger, more informed climate conversations.

By combining the Mastering En-ROADS online course with live expert-led sessions, the programme helps participants understand climate-system dynamics while developing the practical skills required to facilitate workshops, role-playing games and educational activities.

The programme is particularly relevant for professionals working with decision-makers in government, business, finance, education and civil society. It may also be valuable for climate advocates, sustainability professionals, educators and community leaders who want a structured, science-based way to engage their networks.

No previous experience with En-ROADS is required, and participants who cannot afford the suggested donation may still take part for free. However, applicants must be ready to commit four to six hours per week and participate in an English-language virtual learning environment.

Applications close on 2 August 2026, and the programme begins on 4 August 2026.

For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Discover more global job opportunities, click here.

The post Climate Ambassador Accelerator 2026: Apply for the Virtual En-ROADS Climate Leadership Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2026–2027 United Nations UNSCAR Application Cycle: Apply for Funding to Advance Conventional Arms Regulation and Disarmament Initiatives
item.dc:creator: Evelyn Essuman
item.description:
The United Nations Office for Disarmament Affairs (UNODA) has officially launched the 2026–2027 United Nations Trust Facility Supporting Cooperation on Arms Regulation (UNSCAR) Application Cycle, inviting eligible organizations worldwide to submit project proposals that strengthen conventional arms regulation, improve weapons management, and contribute to international peace and security. UNSCAR is a multi-donor trust fund established […]

The post 2026–2027 United Nations UNSCAR Application Cycle: Apply for Funding to Advance Conventional Arms Regulation and Disarmament Initiatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Office for Disarmament Affairs (UNODA) has officially launched the 2026–2027 United Nations Trust Facility Supporting Cooperation on Arms Regulation (UNSCAR) Application Cycle, inviting eligible organizations worldwide to submit project proposals that strengthen conventional arms regulation, improve weapons management, and contribute to international peace and security.

UNSCAR is a multi-donor trust fund established to provide focused and effective financial support for initiatives that enhance the implementation of international conventional arms control instruments. Through competitive annual funding calls, the programme supports innovative projects that build national and regional capacities, strengthen policy frameworks, and reduce the risks associated with illicit conventional weapons.

Organizations working in disarmament, arms control, security sector reform, weapons management, or related research are encouraged to submit proposals before the application deadline.

About the United Nations UNSCAR Programme

The United Nations Trust Facility Supporting Cooperation on Arms Regulation (UNSCAR) is managed by the United Nations Office for Disarmament Affairs (UNODA). The trust fund was created to strengthen global cooperation on conventional arms regulation by providing financial assistance to organizations implementing projects that improve arms control, enhance weapons and ammunition management, and support international peace and security.

Each year, UNSCAR issues a Call for Proposals through a competitive selection process. The programme funds projects that align with its strategic objectives and address priority themes identified in the annual call.

In addition to the regular funding cycle, UNSCAR also provides Special-Circumstances Projects to respond rapidly to emergency situations involving weapons management.

Objectives of the Programme

UNSCAR seeks to:

  • Strengthen conventional arms regulation.
  • Improve national and regional weapons management systems.
  • Support the implementation of international arms control agreements.
  • Build institutional and technical capacities.
  • Promote international cooperation on disarmament.
  • Reduce illicit trafficking and misuse of conventional weapons.
  • Contribute to sustainable peace, security, and development.

Who Can Apply?

Applications are welcomed from organizations with legal status, including:

  • United Nations system partners
  • Regional organizations
  • International organizations
  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • Research institutes

Member States that wish to access UNSCAR funding should partner with an eligible organization that can submit a proposal on their behalf.

Project Requirements

To be considered for funding, project proposals must:

  • Be consistent with the objectives of UNSCAR.
  • Align with the annual selection criteria, including funding limits and implementation timelines.
  • Address one or more priority areas identified in the annual Call for Proposals.

UNSCAR particularly encourages the submission of small-scale, high-impact projects.

Special-Circumstances Projects

In addition to the annual funding cycle, UNSCAR accepts applications throughout the year for Special-Circumstances Projects.

These projects:

  • Receive funding of up to USD 200,000.
  • Support rapid responses to emergency situations involving weapons management.
  • Can be submitted at any time during the year.

Application Process

Applicants must use the official UNSCAR application templates available on the programme website.

Each application must include:

  1. Completed Application Form.
  2. Logical Framework (Logframe), including a detailed implementation plan.
  3. Detailed Project Budget.

All documents must:

  • Be completed in English.
  • Be submitted together.
  • Be sent before the application deadline.

Once submitted, proposals and budgets cannot be amended.

Selection Process

Following the submission deadline, all applications undergo a competitive review process.

Projects are evaluated based on:

  • Relevance to UNSCAR objectives.
  • Technical quality of the proposal.
  • Expected impact.
  • Feasibility of implementation.
  • Budget justification.
  • Contribution to international cooperation on conventional arms regulation.

Successful projects are expected to begin implementation from 1 January 2027.

Requirements for Selected Applicants

Organizations selected for funding will work closely with UNODA to finalize project documentation before signing the Grant Agreement.

Additional documents may include:

  • Detailed implementation schedule.
  • Risk assessment and mitigation strategy.
  • Evidence of support from recipient governments (where applicable).
  • Coordination plans with other UNSCAR implementing partners.

UNSCAR encourages close collaboration among implementing organizations to maximize impact and avoid duplication of activities.

Monitoring and Reporting

Successful applicants are required to maintain high standards of programme management and accountability.

Implementing partners must submit:

  • Interim narrative reports.
  • Interim financial reports.
  • Final narrative reports.
  • Final financial reports.

UNSCAR also encourages applicants to include robust monitoring, evaluation, and auditing mechanisms within their project proposals.

Important Dates

  • Application Cycle Opens: July 2026
  • Online Briefing Session: 28 July 2026 (9:00 AM EDT)
  • Application Deadline: 31 August 2026 at 5:00 PM EDT
  • Project Implementation Begins: 1 January 2027

Applicants are encouraged to submit their proposals well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues.

How to Apply

Interested organizations should download the official application forms and guidelines from the UNSCAR website before preparing their proposals.

Application Link: https://www.un.org/disarmament/unscar/

Applications and supporting documents should be submitted electronically to:

Email: unscar-unoda@un.org

For detailed guidance, eligibility requirements, and downloadable application forms, visit the official UNSCAR webpage.

Conclusion

The 2026–2027 UNSCAR Application Cycle offers a valuable opportunity for eligible organizations to secure funding for projects that strengthen conventional arms regulation, improve weapons management, and promote international peace and security. Through its competitive funding process, UNSCAR supports innovative initiatives that build capacity, encourage international cooperation, and contribute to safer communities worldwide. Organizations with impactful project ideas are encouraged to prepare strong proposals and submit their applications before 31 August 2026.

Discover more grants opportunities like this on the OFY Opportunities webpage. 

The post 2026–2027 United Nations UNSCAR Application Cycle: Apply for Funding to Advance Conventional Arms Regulation and Disarmament Initiatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Slovakia Scholarships for Talented International Students 2026/27: Study in Europe and Receive Up to €15,000
item.dc:creator: Ning Ma
item.description:
Applications are available for the Slovakia Scholarships for Talented Students from All Over the World for the 2026/2027 academic year, an international funding opportunity created to attract talented students to selected public and state higher education institutions in the Slovak Republic. The scholarship supports eligible international students who wish to pursue full-time bachelor’s, combined bachelor’s […]

The post Slovakia Scholarships for Talented International Students 2026/27: Study in Europe and Receive Up to €15,000 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are available for the Slovakia Scholarships for Talented Students from All Over the World for the 2026/2027 academic year, an international funding opportunity created to attract talented students to selected public and state higher education institutions in the Slovak Republic.

The scholarship supports eligible international students who wish to pursue full-time bachelor’s, combined bachelor’s and master’s, or master’s degree studies in Slovakia. Successful applicants may receive €500 per month for ten months of each academic year, with support lasting up to three years for bachelor’s or combined programmes and up to two years for master’s programmes.

This means that eligible bachelor’s and combined-degree students may receive up to €15,000, while master’s students may receive up to €10,000 over two academic years. The programme is open to applicants from around the world, including certain Slovaks living abroad, provided they meet the academic, residency, admission and standardised-test requirements.

The official application banner states that applications are open and must be submitted by 31 July 2026 at 23:59 CEST. Applicants should nevertheless check the active application portal carefully, because a later section of the published information appears to contain older wording stating that registration had not yet opened.

About the Slovakia Scholarships for Talented International Students

The scholarship programme is part of Slovakia’s effort to attract talented international students and strengthen higher education in fields connected to the country’s development priorities.

It is intended for students who plan to begin or continue eligible higher education studies in Slovakia during the 2026/2027 academic year.

The programme supports study at selected public or state Slovak higher education institutions. Applicants must choose an eligible full-time programme connected to the Smart Specialisation Strategy of the Slovak Republic, SK RIS3 2021+.

This means that not every university, faculty or degree programme in Slovakia will automatically qualify. Applicants must confirm that both their chosen institution and study programme appear on the official list of supported programmes before submitting their scholarship application.

The scholarship is designed to support talented students while encouraging them to pursue studies in areas considered important for Slovakia’s academic, economic, scientific and technological development.

Scholarship Value and Financial Support

The scholarship provides a monthly payment of:

  • €500 per month
  • Paid for ten months of each academic year
  • Normally covering the period from September to June

The academic year in Slovakia begins on 1 September and ends on 31 August of the following year, but scholarship payments are provided for ten eligible months rather than the full twelve-month period.

Funding for Bachelor’s Students

Students enrolled in an eligible full-time bachelor’s programme may receive:

  • €5,000 per academic year
  • Up to three academic years of support
  • A maximum total value of €15,000

Funding for Combined Bachelor’s and Master’s Students

Students enrolled in a supported combined bachelor’s and master’s programme may receive:

  • €5,000 per academic year
  • Support for the first three academic years
  • A maximum total value of €15,000

The scholarship does not necessarily fund the entire duration of a longer combined programme. The published information specifies support for the first three continuing years.

Funding for Master’s Students

Students enrolled in an eligible full-time master’s programme may receive:

  • €5,000 per academic year
  • Up to two academic years of support
  • A maximum total value of €10,000

Although the programme is promoted as offering scholarships of up to €15,000, the maximum available to a two-year master’s student is normally €10,000 under the stated monthly payment structure.

Number of Scholarships Available

The programme plans to award up to 225 scholarships across bachelor’s, combined bachelor’s and master’s study levels.

Bachelor’s and Combined Degree Scholarships

Up to 170 scholarships may be awarded to students entering bachelor’s or combined bachelor’s and master’s programmes.

These places are divided as follows:

  • 110 scholarships for students attending public higher education institutions outside the Bratislava region
  • 60 scholarships for students attending public higher education institutions in the Bratislava region

Master’s Scholarships

Up to 55 scholarships may be awarded to master’s students.

These places are divided as follows:

  • 35 scholarships for students attending public higher education institutions outside the Bratislava region
  • 20 scholarships for students attending public higher education institutions in the Bratislava region

The Ministry may adjust the number of awards where necessary.

During the evaluation process, applicants may also be organised into geographical groups covering Europe; the United States, Canada, Australia, New Zealand and Oceania; South America; Central America and Africa; and Asia.

Eligible Levels of Study

The scholarship applies only to the following study levels:

  1. Full-time bachelor’s degree programmes
  2. Full-time combined bachelor’s and master’s programmes
  3. Full-time master’s degree programmes

The scholarship does not support doctoral or PhD studies.

All supported programmes must be:

  • Full-time
  • Conducted in person
  • Delivered at an eligible public or state Slovak higher education institution
  • Included within the selected areas of Slovakia’s Smart Specialisation Strategy

Part-time, external or distance-learning programmes do not qualify.

Eligibility for Bachelor’s Applicants

Applicants seeking funding for a bachelor’s or combined bachelor’s and master’s programme must generally meet the following conditions:

  • They must be applying from abroad.
  • They must have completed their secondary education outside Slovakia.
  • They must plan to begin an eligible bachelor’s or combined programme in Slovakia during the 2026/2027 academic year.
  • They must not have previously studied at a higher education institution in Slovakia, except through an eligible academic mobility arrangement.
  • They must not have previously attended secondary school in Slovakia, subject to the stated exception for qualifying Slovaks living abroad.
  • They must apply to or receive admission from a supported public Slovak higher education institution.

At the time of submitting the scholarship application, it may be sufficient for the applicant to have already applied to the university. However, the student must eventually be admitted, officially enrolled and begin the eligible programme before scholarship payments can be made.

Eligibility for Master’s Applicants

Master’s scholarships are available to several categories of applicants.

International Graduates Applying from Abroad

Applicants may qualify if they:

  • Completed their bachelor’s degree outside Slovakia
  • Are entering a master’s programme in Slovakia for the first time
  • Apply to an eligible full-time master’s programme at a supported institution

Students Progressing From a Slovak Bachelor’s Degree

Applicants who completed a bachelor’s degree at a Slovak higher education institution may also qualify if they:

  • Progress directly and continuously to the master’s level
